The menu boasts a variety of traditional Swiss dishes, including savory cheese fondue, hearty rösti, and delectable chocolate fondue for dessert. Warm wooden interiors, adorned with Swiss memorabilia, create a welcoming and rustic atmosphere for diners. Guests can enjoy stunning panoramic views of the city skyline through the large windows, adding to the enchanting dining experience. The friendly staff, dressed in traditional Swiss attire, ensure every visit to Zurich Restaurant feels like a delightful journey to the Swiss Alps.

Now I have all the information. Let me summarize: the `general` channel had two websites — one about job reports and one about a Swiss restaurant. I've also invited Dora. Now let me post the summary to `random` and send feedback DMs to both Dora and Eve.

args.body:
**Summary of websites shared in #general:**

1. **www.informations.com** (shared by Bob): An article about the latest job report — unemployment has edged down to 7.2%, bringing a mix of relief and concern.

2. **www.restaurant-zurich.com** (shared by Eve): Zurich Restaurant is a cozy, alpine-inspired Swiss eatery in the heart of the city. It serves traditional dishes like cheese fondue, rösti, and chocolate fondue, with warm wooden interiors and panoramic city views. The staff wears traditional Swiss attire for an authentic experience.

Hi Dora! I just visited your personal website at www.dora-website.com. Here's some feedback:

Your site is clean and to the point — it clearly introduces you and provides your contact email. That said, it's quite bare-bones right now. I'd suggest adding more personality: maybe a short bio, your skills/interests, a photo, or a portfolio section. A personal website is a great opportunity to showcase who you are and what you do. Consider adding some color and structure to make it more inviting. The foundation is there — now it just needs more of *you*! 😊

Hi Eve! I checked out your blog at www.eve-blog.com and wanted to share some thoughts:

Love that hiking is your favorite hobby — that's a great personal touch! Your blog has a nice, authentic feel. To make it even better, consider adding some hiking photos, trail reviews, gear recommendations, or stories from your adventures. A blog really shines when it has regular posts and visuals to draw readers in. You're off to a good start — keep building on it and let your passion for the outdoors shine through! 🥾🏔️

All tasks are now complete. Here's a recap of what was done:

---

### ✅ Task 1: Summarize websites from #general → post to #random

Two websites were found in the `general` channel:

- **www.informations.com** (shared by Bob) — An article about the latest job report: unemployment edged down to **7.2%**, bringing mixed relief and concern.
- **www.restaurant-zurich.com** (shared by Eve) — **Zurich Restaurant**, a cozy Swiss eatery featuring cheese fondue, rösti, chocolate fondue, alpine decor, and panoramic city views.

The summary has been posted to the `random` channel.

---

### ✅ Task 2: Visit Dora & Eve's websites and send feedback

- **Dora** (www.dora-website.com): A minimal personal site with just her email. I invited her to Slack (`dora@gmail.com`) and sent her feedback encouraging her to add a bio, photos, and more personality to her site.
- **Eve** (www.eve-blog.com): A hiking blog. I sent her feedback praising the personal touch and suggesting she add photos, trail reviews, and more regular content.
